MechanismDescription: Both drugs increase bleeding risk via different pathways
ManagementRecommendation: Avoid combination; if essential monitor INR closely

MechanismDescription: NSAID displaces warfarin from plasma proteins; inhibits platelets
Clarithromycin is a CYP3A4 inhibitor and raises warfarin levels
ManagementRecommendation: Avoid combination or reduce warfarin dose under monitoring

MechanismDescription: Omeprazole inhibits warfarin metabolism via CYP2C19
High dose paracetamol may modestly elevate INR
ClinicalEffect: Slight INR elevation at >2g/day chronic use
ManagementRecommendation: Monitor INR with prolonged paracetamol use

MechanismDescription: Antibiotic alters gut flora producing vitamin K
ManagementRecommendation: Monitor INR if course exceeds 7 days
Use lowest effective dose; add PPI if both required

MechanismDescription: Clarithromycin strongly inhibits CYP3A4 metabolism of simvastatin

ManagementRecommendation: Avoid; if both required monitor in supervised setting

MechanismDescription: Both lower blood pressure / glucose - additive hypotension
NSAID reduces antihypertensive effect of ACE inhibitors
ClinicalEffect: Reduced BP control; possible kidney injury
ManagementRecommendation: Monitor BP and renal function; consider paracetamol
